financial.revenue: No numeric FY27 revenue guidance (Coforge's policy); management stated 'FY27 is poised to deliver on that promise of exceptional performance' with continued industry-leading growth. FY26 actuals: revenue ₹16,420.7 Cr (+35.9% INR, +29.2% USD); Q4 revenue ₹4,450 Cr (+28.7% CC YoY, +2.0% CC QoQ)
financial.growth: Qualitative: industry-leading double-digit organic growth in FY27, underwritten by a $1.752 Bn next-12-month executable order book (+16.4% YoY) and Encora cross-sell; note a planned closure of a $20 Mn low-margin India portfolio will trim optical growth
financial.margins: FY27 guidance: consolidated EBITDA margin 20.5-21% (vs FY26 18.6% blended; Q4 exit 20.5%); EBIT margin exited Q4 at 16.6% (+370 bps YoY for FY26); FCF/PAT conversion guided above the earlier 70-80% band from FY27 (Q4 normalized FCF/PAT was 156%)
financial.capex: Not material/not disclosed separately ₹ people business; spend directed at AI practice, delivery infrastructure and G&A consolidation (20-25% G&A synergies targeted in the combined business including Encora)

business.expansion: Encora acquisition integration complete (expands addressable client wallet, product-engineering depth); Cigniti merger absorbed; structural G&A reset done quietly through FY26 ('structurally reset the margin profile for FY27 and beyond'); dedicated AI practice under a named AI head
business.products: Full-stack IT services: BFS, insurance, travel/transportation/hospitality (historic specialisms), public sector, healthcare; product engineering (Encora), digital assurance (Cigniti), AI/GenAI services; Pega/cloud/data partnerships
business.geography: Americas-led with strong UK/EMEA and APAC; exact FY26 split not in reviewed excerpts; large deals signed across North America, UK, APAC

commitments: FCF/PAT conversion above the prior 70-80% guidance from FY27 onwards
Deliver 'exceptional' FY27 performance on record executable order book
Realize 20-25% G&A synergies across the combined business (incl. Encora)
risks: Integration execution across two large acquisitions (Cigniti done, Encora just completed) - Medium
Demand environment described as 'very challenging' for the industry - Medium
Deliberate $20 Mn low-margin India portfolio exit creates a small optical growth drag - Low
Client concentration building at the top (one client now >$100 Mn) - Low/Medium

guidance.summary: Coforge closed FY26 with revenue up 29.2% in USD to ₹16,421 Cr and PAT up 91.6% to ₹1,556 Cr, and guided FY27 EBITDA margins to 20.5-21% on a record $1.752 Bn executable order book (+16.4%).

deal_wins: Five large deals signed in Q4 FY26 (across BFS, travel, insurance, public sector); $648 Mn total Q4 order intake
Mix of new large accounts: five BFS, three travel, one insurance, one public sector

order_book.total_value: $1.752 Bn executable over the next 12 months (+16.4% YoY) ₹ Coforge's signature visibility metric
order_book.execution_timeline: 100% executable within 12 months by definition; multi-year TCV beyond this not disclosed
order_book.by_segment: Not split by vertical in disclosure

deals_and_pipeline.sales_pipeline: Not quantified; management cited strong large-deal pipeline and expanded addressable wallet post-Encora

customer_additions: New large-account adds spanning BFS (5), travel (3), insurance (1), public sector (1)
customer_losses: None disclosed; $20 Mn India portfolio exit is voluntary
customers.customer_concentration: Client pyramid (newly disclosed): 1 client >$100 Mn; 3 at $50-100 Mn; 9 at $20-50 Mn; 23 at $10-20 Mn; 42 at $5-10 Mn; 167 at $1-5 Mn ₹ healthy distribution, modest top-end concentration
customers.net_assessment: Net positive ₹ the pyramid is fattening in the middle ($10-50 Mn bands), the classic signature of durable mining; Encora adds a complementary client set for cross-sell

revenue_by_segment.BFS (banking & financial services): Largest vertical | growth 'broad-based and strong' (exact % not disclosed)

segments.segment_outlook: All key industry units performed strongly in FY26; BFS and travel lead the large-deal mix into FY27; product engineering (Encora) is the new growth vector

geography.expansion_plans: Deepen North America via Encora; large-deal pursuit across NA/UK/APAC; no new-country specifics

headcount.hiring_plan: Not disclosed numerically; net additions tracking demand
headcount.utilization_target: Utilization discussed on call (level not captured in reviewed excerpt); efficiency gains embedded in the margin reset
headcount.attrition_rate: Not captured in reviewed excerpt (historically ~11-12% LTM, among industry's best)

raw_materials.cost_outlook: Not applicable ₹ people business; wage costs and pyramid optimization are the levers; G&A consolidation is the FY27 margin driver
raw_materials.expected_benefit: FY27 EBITDA margin +~190-250 bps vs FY26 blended (to 20.5-21%) from G&A synergies, Encora/Cigniti integration, low-margin exits

working_capital.cash_conversion_cycle: Improving ₹ FY26 FCF +68%; Q4 normalized FCF/PAT 156%
working_capital.cash_release_potential: Structural: FCF/PAT conversion guided above 70-80% from FY27

capex_detailed.funding_source: Internal accruals; acquisition funding (Encora) structure not detailed in reviewed excerpts

debt_management.net_debt_target: Not disclosed; acquisition-related leverage being serviced from sharply rising FCF

market_share.by_segment: Top-tier challenger among Indian mid-cap IT; #1 or #2 mindshare in travel tech and strong BFS/insurance niches; Encora adds product-engineering share
market_share.overall_trend: Strengthening decisively ₹ 29.2% USD growth in a year when tier-1 Indian IT grew low single digits is dramatic share capture

competitive_position.pricing_strategy: Value/outcome-led with large-deal constructs; margin expansion alongside hypergrowth indicates pricing discipline rather than buy-the-business behavior
competitive_advantages: Proven large-deal engine: 21 large deals in FY26, $648 Mn intake in Q4 alone
Executable-order-book model gives best-in-class 12-month revenue visibility ($1.752 Bn, +16.4%)
Structural margin reset (G&A consolidation + Encora/Cigniti synergies) delivering growth AND margin simultaneously
competitive_threats: GenAI-driven deflation in traditional services pricing industry-wide (Medium/High)
Tier-1s (Accenture, TCS, Infosys) competing harder for the same mega-deals as discretionary spend tightens (Medium)
Integration fatigue risk from back-to-back large acquisitions (Medium)

guidance.investor_verdict: FY26's 29% USD growth with 92% PAT growth is the best growth-margin combination in Indian IT, and the FY27 setup (20.5-21% EBITDA guide, $1.75 Bn executable book, completed integrations) makes Coforge the sector's momentum standard-bearer ₹ the debate is purely about the multiple, not the execution.

analyst_dimensions.valuation_context: Trades at a premium to mid-cap IT peers (~35-40x forward range) justified by growth 3-4x the industry rate; brokerage targets ₹1,670-2,100 post-results; FY26 EPS ~₹47-50 implies the premium persists only while 20%+ growth does
analyst_dimensions.consensus_gap: Q4 beat across the board ₹ PAT ₹615 Cr vs muted expectations (+145% QoQ), margins ahead; FY27 EBITDA guide of 20.5-21% was above most street models (~19.5-20%), driving target upgrades (Nomura, Geojit positive)
analyst_dimensions.achievability: Strong ₹ management's Q3 promise of a 'very successful FY26' was delivered precisely; the margin guide is underwritten by already-executed G&A actions rather than hoped-for leverage; revenue momentum is contractually visible via the executable book
analyst_dimensions.margin_quality: High quality and improving structurally: +431 bps FY26 EBITDA expansion came from integration synergies, pyramid/G&A reset and mix, not one-offs; the disclosed exit of low-margin India work shows deliberate quality curation; sustainability hinges on wage cycles and deal pricing
analyst_dimensions.capex_roi: Asset-light; the 'capex' is M&A ₹ Cigniti and Encora are already accretive (margin reset attributed partly to combined-entity synergies), an unusually fast payback for Indian IT acquisitions
analyst_dimensions.order_book_health: $1.752 Bn 12-month executable (+16.4%) ₹ ~85-88% of implied FY27 revenue already visible ₹ the best forward coverage in the peer group; risk is conversion timing, not demand
analyst_dimensions.risk_opportunity_scorecard: Opportunities: Encora cross-sell into a fattening client pyramid (high prob/high impact), AI practice monetization (medium/medium), continued large-deal share gains from tier-1s (high/medium). Risks: GenAI pricing deflation (medium/medium), integration distraction (low-medium/medium), premium multiple compression on any growth wobble (medium/high), top-client concentration (low/low)
analyst_dimensions.management_credibility: Excellent ₹ Sudhir Singh's team has met or beaten its stated trajectory for multiple consecutive years, pre-announced the FY26/FY27 arc on the Q3 call and delivered, and added disclosure (client pyramid) rather than removing it; among the most credible narrators in Indian IT
analyst_dimensions.forward_estimate_impact: FY27E: revenue ~₹19,500-20,500 Cr (+19-25% INR), EBITDA ~₹4,100-4,300 Cr at 20.75% midpoint, PAT ~₹2,200-2,400 Cr (EPS growth ~40-55% with margin flow-through) ₹ street EPS upgrades of 5-10% followed the print and more likely as Encora synergies quantify
analyst_dimensions.quarterly_trajectory: Q1 FY27 (Jul 2026): first clean post-integration quarter ₹ margin walk toward 21% begins; H1: Encora cross-sell evidence, large-deal cadence (~$500-650 Mn/qtr intake needed); H2: operating leverage compounding; catalysts ₹ each quarter's executable-book print, any mega-deal announcement, AI practice revenue disclosure

insights.thesis: The standout growth-and-margin compounder in Indian IT — FY26 delivered 29.2% CC revenue growth with EBIT margin up 370bps to 14.4% and PAT up 92%, with management guiding 20.5%+ EBITDA for FY27 — the rare mid-cap where both levers (growth and profitability) are expanding simultaneously off a $1.75bn executable order book.
insights.trigger: Downgrade to HOLD if Encora integration (₹22,194 cr acquisition closed Apr-2026) shows revenue attrition or margin dilution beyond two quarters, or if quarterly order intake falls below ~$400mn signaling deal pipeline exhaustion; the FY27 EBITDA guidance of 20.5%+ is the single accountability metric to track quarterly.

valuation.metric: ~₹1,169/share, mcap ~₹78,000 cr → trailing P/E ~50x on FY26 PAT ₹1,556 cr; vs Persistent ~55-60x, LTIMindtree ~30x, TCS/Infosys ~22-25x, Nifty ~22x; broker targets ₹1,670-2,100 post-results
valuation.assessment: Optically expensive at 50x trailing, but the trailing multiple is misleading when PAT just grew 92% — on FY27 (full-year of expanded margins + Encora consolidation) the forward multiple compresses fast; among mid-caps, Coforge has the best growth-adjusted multiple given it's growing 3-4x the Tier-1 rate

growth.metric: FY26 revenue $1.87bn, +29.2% CC / +35.9% INR; Q4 +21.2% USD YoY; FY26 order intake $2,262mn with 21 large deals; 12-month executable order book $1.75bn +16.4% YoY; top-5 client revenue +45.8%
growth.assessment: Industry-leading growth with breadth — top accounts growing faster than the company (mining success), large-deal machine consistent across quarters, and Sabre-deal execution proving it can absorb mega-engagements; Encora adds digital-engineering scale on top. Sustainability is supported by the order book covering ~75%+ of forward revenue

profitability.metric: FY26 EBITDA margin up 431bps, EBIT margin 14.4% (+370bps); Q4 EBITDA margin 20.6% (+232bps QoQ); FY27 guidance: EBITDA >20.5% full-year; revenue per head $71.8K rising; utilization 82.5%
profitability.assessment: The margin expansion is structural, not cyclical — driven by AI-led delivery productivity, pyramid optimization, exit of low-margin India business, and operating leverage on large deals; guiding to hold Q4's exit margin for all of FY27 is aggressive but management has met guidance for multiple consecutive years

cash_strength.metric: Historically strong OCF conversion and net-cash operations; the Encora acquisition (~₹22,194 cr, closed Apr-23-2026) is the balance-sheet event — likely funded via debt + equity, materially changing leverage from FY27
cash_strength.assessment: Pre-Encora the balance sheet was clean; post-Encora, Coforge carries meaningful acquisition financing for the first time at this scale. Cash generation (EBITDA ~$350mn+ and rising) can service it, but deleveraging pace and any equity dilution are now live monitorables — this is the one dimension that got worse, deliberately

catalyst.metric: Encora consolidation from Q1 FY27 (revenue scale jump toward $2.5bn+ run-rate); FY27 EBITDA guidance delivery (20.5%+) quarter by quarter; continued large-deal announcements; potential index weight increases on enlarged market cap and free float
catalyst.assessment: Near-term catalysts are quarterly proof points on guidance — each clean quarter compounds the re-rating; Encora is double-edged but if integration lands, Coforge leapfrogs into the LTIMindtree/Mphasis scale tier with better growth, forcing institutional re-benchmarking within 12 months

moat.metric: Deep vertical specialization (insurance/Duck Creek, travel/Sabre-class platforms, BFS), 10.8% attrition — lowest in industry, AI-native delivery toolchain driving the margin gap, repeatable large-deal pursuit engine, top-10 clients at only 31% (low concentration risk for its size)
moat.assessment: Mid-cap IT moats come from vertical depth + execution culture rather than scale, and Coforge has the strongest version of both — lowest attrition in the industry is the hardest metric to fake and directly feeds delivery quality and margin; the AI-productivity lead is real but will be competed away over 2-3 years, so the durable moat is the vertical IP

opportunity.metric: AI-led vendor consolidation is shifting share from Tier-1s to agile challengers on large deals; Encora opens product-engineering/digital-native client base (a segment Coforge lacked); $2bn+ revenue scale unlocks mega-deal eligibility previously gated by size
opportunity.assessment: The under-appreciated piece is the share-shift dynamic — enterprises restructuring IT spend around AI are re-tendering incumbents' work, and Coforge's win rate on those re-tenders is what produced 29% growth in a 4-6% industry; the market still models mean reversion to industry growth, which five consecutive years of outperformance argues against

sector_briefing.sector_outlook: Neutral for the sector aggregate, bullish for share-gainers. Tier-1 Indian IT is growing low-single-digits as GenAI deflates time-and-materials revenue and clients consolidate vendors; but that same disruption is re-opening locked accounts, and the spoils are flowing to AI-native mid-caps that price outcomes rather than headcount. Discretionary spend is recovering unevenly — BFSI and travel verticals (Coforge's strongholds) are among the firmer pockets.
sector_briefing.sector_growth: Indian IT services industry growing ~4-6%; the winning mid-cap cohort (Coforge, Persistent, Hexaware) compounding 15-30% by taking share; industry margins bifurcating — firms with AI delivery leverage are expanding margins (Coforge +370bps in one year) while labor-arbitrage-dependent players compress; M&A consolidation accelerating as scale becomes necessary for mega-deals (Coforge-Cigniti, Coforge-Encora, Hexaware roll-ups).
sector_briefing.company_positioning: Coforge is the fastest-growing scaled IT services company in India and arguably the best large-deal execution machine in the mid-cap tier — 21 large deals in FY26 and an order book covering most of forward-year revenue. Versus peers: Persistent matches its growth but at a higher multiple with thinner margins; LTIMindtree has scale but half the growth; the Encora acquisition deliberately closes Coforge's product-engineering gap versus Persistent/GlobalLogic. Its attrition (10.8%) leading the industry signals the culture is scaling with the P&L.
sector_briefing.sector_risks: (1) GenAI revenue deflation — Coforge is on the right side currently (margins expanding from AI productivity) but pricing pressure eventually reaches everyone; outcome-based contracts are the hedge. (2) US macro/BFSI spending shock — Coforge's BFS and insurance concentration cuts both ways; a US recession would hit its largest verticals first. (3) Encora integration risk — ₹22,000 cr is the largest deal in company history; cultural attrition or client churn at Encora is the most probable thesis-breaker. (4) Currency: INR strength compresses margins exactly when guidance (20.5% EBITDA) leaves limited buffer. (5) Multiple risk: at ~50x trailing, any growth deceleration gets punished disproportionately — position sizing should respect that.
